workflow: Before driving a persisted-state browser workflow (e.g. reload persistence), the loop resolves the strongest clean-state isolation the surface supports and records it as evidence, never asserting against storage that may carry rows from a previous run (the validation run's duplicate Coffee beans rows)

steps.label: compile
steps.result: compiles clean; executable bit set, consistent with review_close_path.py / handoff_required.py / record_human_uat.py

steps.label: clean-context-surface
steps.result: strategy=clean-context; reset_snippet=None; plan names Playwright newContext / Puppeteer --user-data-dir / Chrome --user-data-dir $(mktemp -d) and closing the throwaway context

steps.label: reset-surface-named-keys-and-indexeddb
steps.result: strategy=reset; reset_snippet emits localStorage.clear(), sessionStorage.clear(), removeItem('cart'/'beans'), indexedDB.deleteDatabase('beansDB'), location.reload(); warns IndexedDB delete is async

steps.label: unique-data-surface
steps.result: strategy=unique-data; generates per-run token (e.g. 20260620T015030Z-e0ba); plan instructs asserting on 'Coffee beans UAT-<token>' so accumulated rows cannot create a false pass; flags dedup-on-label as insufficient

steps.label: escalate-under-strict-exits-nonzero
steps.result: strategy=escalate; plan says block for human UAT and name 'clean browser profile or explicit storage reset' as required capability; exit code 1 — the gate the loop needs so it does not submit against polluted storage

steps.label: escalate-without-strict-exits-zero
steps.result: exit code 0 — planning/dry-run path does not hard-fail, only --strict gates

steps.label: strategy-override-honored
steps.result: strategy=unique-data; source notes 'override (--strategy); surface=reset' — operator can pin a weaker strategy when intentionally avoiding reset

steps.label: strategy-downgrade-when-surface-too-weak
steps.result: downgrades to escalate with WARNING 'clean-context requires surface clean-context but surface is none; downgrading' — manifest never claims an isolation the browser cannot deliver

steps.label: manifest-write-and-evidence-log-append
steps.action: two consecutive writes against a temp artifacts-dir; inspect browser-uat.json + evidence.log
steps.result: writes uat-artifacts/<issue>/browser-uat.json (latest) and appends one JSON line per run to evidence.log (2 lines after 2 runs); on-disk manifest stays clean (manifest_path added to descriptor only AFTER write)

steps.label: issue-id-guard
steps.result: rejects path-separator issue id; exit 1 — prevents escaping the per-issue artifact dir, matching record_human_uat.py

steps.label: prose-fix-skill
steps.action: grep SKILL.md '## Persisted-State Browser UAT' + Loop Contract step 7 cross-reference
steps.result: new section present after Screenshot Artifact Strategy; step 7 bullet added pointing reload-persistence workflows at it; escalate routes into Human Escalation Protocol

steps.label: prose-fix-readme-and-config
steps.action: grep README.md 'browser_uat_isolation.py' + 'Persisted-state browser UAT starts from a clean state'; grep references/config.md loop-guarantee note
steps.result: helper listed in layout; guarantee bullet added alongside the sibling follow-up guarantees; config.md notes it as a loop guarantee (not a config knob), mirroring the handoff guarantee note

notes: UAT is CLI/functional: this issue changes a Codex skill's guidance + adds a no-deps helper script. There is no live browser surface to drive in this repo, so the failure mode (validation: reload persistence UAT accumulated duplicate Coffee beans rows because localStorage reset was unavailable) is addressed by resolving+recording a strategy deterministically and gating on --strict rather than by running a browser workflow.

The helper deliberately NEVER mutates browser state from the CLI (it cannot); it emits the exact JS/commands and records the chosen strategy, mirroring review_close_path.py (resolve a path) + record_human_uat.py (write evidence).
install.sh uses cp -R of the repo, so scripts/browser_uat_isolation.py ships with the skill; executable bit set, consistent with the other helpers.

Strategy precedence encodes 'strongest acceptable isolation first' (clean-context > reset > unique-data > escalate), matching the acceptance criteria's three named options plus a human-escalation fallback when none is available.
